We want to find functions $p(t), q(t), f(t)$ such that

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item $p$ and $q$ are continuous functions on the open interval $(0, \pi)$.
\item $f$ is an infinitely differentiable nonzero function on the whole real
      line $(-\infty, \infty)$ such that $f(0) = f'(0) = f''(0)$.
\item $y = \sin t$ and $y = f(t)$ are solutions of the differential equation
      $y'' + p(t)y' + q(t)y = 0$ on $(0, \pi)$.
\end{enumerate}

Is this possible? Either prove this is not possible, or show this is possible
by providing an explicit example of such $f, p, q$.
